renewal in November at risk unless pricing and feature gaps are resolved", "raised_by": "Marcus, VP Operations at Globex Inc"}, "natural": "Marcus from Globex Inc has raised that their team is frustrated with slow reporting export performance and unaddressed SAML setup, and that renewal in November is at risk unless pricing and feature gaps are resolved.", "confidence": 0.85, "scope_actors": [], "scope_entities": [], "scope_temporal": {"valid_from": "2026-04-22T09:08:51.663467+00:00", "valid_until": null}, "falsifier": {"kind": "observation_pattern", "pattern": "Globex confirms they are satisfied with progress on reporting export and SAML and no longer considering alternatives", "within_window": "P6M"}}}], "reasoning_trace": "The triggering event is an email from Marcus (VP Ops, Globex Inc) — an external actor (actor_id=external). The email contains two distinct claims: (1) Globex is actively evaluating two competing vendors this quarter and renewal is contingent on resolving pricing and feature gaps — this is a state assertion about the customer's posture. (2) The team is frustrated with slow reporting export and unaddressed SAML, and renewal is at risk — this is a concern raised by Marcus. Both are already captured in existing models (019db473-90ef-7000-bcca-f2eb092daa43 and 019db473-359c-7000-9a53-893d64973b5b) but those models have empty scope_actors and scope_entities, making them invisible to the system.
